rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the paper's thesis, contribution framing, and positioning at a level above individual sentences.
rationale.R1.2: A general opinion on the excerpt would centrally include whether the optimization claims, analysis consequences, and positioning follow coherently.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical and theoretical support claims, so checking whether reported evidence supports those claims is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: Because the excerpt explicitly compares AdaSpec to SARAH, SPIDER, SpiderBoost, and Fang et al. (2018), novelty assessment is central.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation naturally includes judgment of clarity, flow, register, and concision in the presented research prose.
rationale.R1.7: Named prior work and lower-bound attribution make citation coverage and attribution accuracy a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of responsibility for an open-ended research judgment must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims about optimality, robustness, and method-of-choice status, so avoiding overclaiming is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within projecting responsibilities rather than actually critiquing or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for possible downstream harm from unsupported research, benchmark, or deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation wording and the specific claims present in the artifact.
